title: Implement Metadata Setters And Status Moves
description: Add `spec set status`, `spec set feature-root`, `spec set tags`, `spec set priority`, and `spec set description`, ensuring only the targeted field changes and status updates move the file into the matching lifecycle directory.
acceptance_criteria: Each `spec set` subcommand updates only its intended frontmatter field and leaves all other content unchanged.
`spec set status <draft|planned|done>` updates the `status` field and moves the file to the matching folder.
Move failures are caught and reported with the specified clean error message.
Running any `spec set *` on a legacy spec without frontmatter exits with the required `spec migrate` guidance.

handoff_summary.completed: Verified complete implementation of cmd_set (lines 460-532) and corresponding parser subcommands (lines 607-636) in skills/spec-management/spec.py. No code changes required.
handoff_summary.next_action: Proceed to tester bead for spec.py set subcommand coverage.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — file-scope claim on skills/spec-management/spec.py is now released.

conflict_risks: None — file-scope claim on skills/spec-management/spec.py is now released.

execution_history.summary: Deferred due to file-scope conflict with active bead B-9ba25868
Deferred due to file-scope conflict with active bead B-4cb47105

All five `spec set` subcommands (status, feature-root, tags, priority, description) are fully implemented in skills/spec-management/spec.py. The implementation was present in the feature branch when this bead started — a prior bead appears to have included it. All four acceptance criteria are verified: each setter modifies only its targeted frontmatter field; `spec set status` moves the file to the matching lifecycle directory; `OSError` on move is caught with the exact required message; legacy specs (no frontmatter) exit with `spec migrate` guidance. Syntax check passes.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
